title: potentilla subacaulis var. cinerea
intro: Potentilla subacaulis var. cinerea, also known as cinereous dwarf cinquefoil, is a low-growing, hairy perennial herb belonging to the rose family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Potentilla subacaulis var. cinerea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit protective effects against oxidative stress and cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and may be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
